Yeah, so my best advice for anybody who is like cool, I've been using chatGPT, I wanna really start using this seriously in my business is to go learn the playground, which you can access by going to beta.openai.com slash playground. You need to create an account with OpenAI if you don't already have it. It does cost money, so you need to like put in I think payment information and then they give you $18 ish of free credits, which is like probably hundreds of requests. So you do have like enough to play with to go and get started. But what it does when you go to the playground is you get direct access to the AI model that powers chatGPT, like the actual kind of core of it, which is called GPT-3. And it is slightly different. So like the difference between the two, right? It's like chatGPT was, you took that core model and then you made it really, really good at chatting with people or dialogue. And so when people get into the playground, they'll notice immediately, it's a little bit less of a conversational interface. In fact, it's like just a box and you just write your prompt and then you click submit and then it generates the response. But once you start getting familiar with the playground and that way to interact with these AI models, then you can take that and use it in an API in like any tool that you're using that has an API connection.
